description: As a TUI developer, I want a Field interface so that I can create consistent, composable form components
steps: Create pkg/tui/field.go with Field interface
Interface includes Init, Update, View methods (Bubble Tea Model)
Interface includes Focus, Blur lifecycle methods
Interface includes IsComplete, IsCancelled, Error, Skip state methods
Interface includes WithTheme, WithWidth, WithHeight configuration methods
Interface includes GetKey, GetValue accessor methods
Verify: Code compiles with no errors

description: As a TUI developer, I want a Wizard container so that I can orchestrate multi-step form flows
steps: Create pkg/tui/wizard.go with Wizard struct
Wizard holds slice of Steps and tracks current index
Implement Init that returns focus command for first step
Implement Update that delegates to current step's field
Implement View that renders only current step
Verify: Wizard compiles and can hold Steps

description: As a TUI developer, I want a Context struct so that components can access shared state without tight coupling
steps: Create pkg/tui/context.go with Context struct
Add Width, Height int fields for terminal dimensions
Add Theme pointer field
Add State *WorkflowState field
Add GitService and JiraService interface fields
Verify: Context can be instantiated and fields accessed

description: As a TUI developer, I want a WorkflowState struct so that data collected across wizard steps is accessible
steps: Define WorkflowState struct in context.go
Add WorkflowType string field
Add WorktreeName, BranchName, BaseBranch string fields
Add JiraIssue pointer field (can be nil)
Verify: WorkflowState can store and retrieve all workflow data

description: As a TUI developer, I want a Theme struct so that visual styles are centralized and configurable
steps: Create pkg/tui/theme.go with Theme struct
Add Focused and Blurred FieldStyles structs
FieldStyles includes lipgloss.Style for Title, Description, Input, Error
Create DefaultTheme() function returning usable defaults
Verify: DefaultTheme returns non-nil styles that render visibly

description: As a TUI developer, I want a Step struct so that I can configure fields with names and conditional skip logic
steps: Define Step struct in wizard.go
Add Name string field for identification
Add Field field of type Field interface
Add Skip func(*WorkflowState) bool field (can be nil)
Verify: Step can be created with and without Skip function

description: As a user, I want to see a list of options so that I can choose one
steps: Create pkg/tui/fields/selector.go with Selector struct
NewSelector accepts title string and options slice
View renders title text above options
View renders each option on its own line
View shows cursor indicator (▸) on current selection
Verify: Run selector and see title with options listed vertically

description: As a user, I want to navigate options with arrow keys so that I can browse choices
steps: Handle tea.KeyMsg for 'up' arrow in Update
Handle tea.KeyMsg for 'down' arrow in Update
Up arrow decrements selection index (wrap to bottom at top)
Down arrow increments selection index (wrap to top at bottom)
Verify: Press up/down arrows and see cursor move through all options

description: As a user, I want to navigate options with vim keys so that I can browse choices
steps: Handle tea.KeyMsg for 'j' key in Update
Handle tea.KeyMsg for 'k' key in Update
K key decrements selection index (wrap to bottom at top)
J key increments selection index (wrap to top at bottom)
Verify: Press j/k keys and see cursor move through all options

description: As a user, I want to confirm my selection with Enter so that I can proceed
steps: Handle tea.KeyMsg for 'enter' in Update
Enter sets selected value to current option
Enter sets internal complete flag to true
Enter returns nextStepMsg command
Verify: Press Enter, confirm IsComplete() returns true and GetValue() returns selected option

description: As a TUI developer, I want Selector to implement Field interface so that it integrates with Wizard
steps: Implement Focus() returning tea.Cmd (can be nil)
Implement Blur() returning tea.Cmd (can be nil)
Implement IsComplete() returning bool
Implement GetValue() returning selected option
Implement WithTheme() returning configured Selector
Verify: Selector satisfies Field interface (compiler check)

description: As a user, I want to see a text input above a list so that I can type to filter or enter custom values
steps: Create pkg/tui/fields/filterable.go with Filterable struct
NewFilterable accepts title, description, and options
View renders title and description at top
View renders text input with placeholder
View renders options list below input
Verify: Run filterable and see input field above list of options

description: As a user, I want the list to filter as I type so that I can find items quickly
steps: Capture typed characters in text input
Filter options where label contains input text (case-insensitive)
Update displayed list to show only matching options
Show 'No matches' message when filter returns empty
Clear filter shows all options again
Verify: Type partial text, see list shrink to matches; clear input, see all options

description: As a user, I want to navigate filtered results with arrow keys so that I can select from matches
steps: Handle up/down arrows when filtered list is non-empty
Arrow keys move selection through filtered items only
Selection cursor updates visually
Text input keeps focus (can continue typing)
Verify: Filter to subset, use arrows to navigate through filtered results

description: As a user, I want to select a list item with Enter so that I can choose a suggested option
steps: Handle Enter when a list item is highlighted
Set value to highlighted item's value (not label)
Set complete flag to true
Return nextStepMsg command
Verify: Navigate to item, press Enter, confirm GetValue() returns item's value

description: As a user, I want to enter a custom value with Enter so that I'm not limited to suggestions
steps: Handle Enter when no list item selected (empty list or no highlight)
Use trimmed text input as the value
Set complete flag to true
Return nextStepMsg command
Verify: Type custom text not in list, press Enter, confirm GetValue() returns typed text

description: As a TUI developer, I want Filterable to implement Field interface so that it integrates with Wizard
steps: Implement Focus() that focuses the text input
Implement Blur() returning tea.Cmd
Implement IsComplete(), GetValue(), WithTheme()
Verify: Filterable satisfies Field interface (compiler check)

description: As a user, I want to see an editable text field so that I can enter or modify text
steps: Create pkg/tui/fields/textinput.go with TextInput struct
NewTextInput accepts title and description
View renders title and description
View renders text input with visible cursor
View shows current text content
Verify: Run textinput and see title with editable input area

description: As a user, I want to type and edit text so that I can enter my desired value
steps: Handle character key events to insert text at cursor
Handle backspace to delete character before cursor
Handle left/right arrows to move cursor position
Display updates immediately on each keystroke
Verify: Type text, use backspace, move cursor with arrows, see changes

description: As a user, I want to see a pre-filled default value so that I can accept or modify a suggestion
steps: Add WithDefault(string) configuration method
On Focus, populate input with default value
Position cursor at end of default text
User can edit, clear, or accept default
Verify: Create with default, focus field, see default text, edit it

description: As a user, I want to submit my text with Enter so that I can proceed
steps: Handle Enter key in Update
If validation configured, run validator on current text
If valid (or no validator), set complete=true and return nextStepMsg
If invalid, set error and remain on field
Verify: Enter submits; with validator, invalid input shows error

description: As a TUI developer, I want TextInput to implement Field interface so that it integrates with Wizard
steps: Implement Focus(), Blur() methods
Implement Error() returning current validation error
Implement IsComplete(), GetValue(), WithTheme()
Verify: TextInput satisfies Field interface (compiler check)

description: As a user, I want to see Yes/No options with a summary so that I can confirm an action
steps: Create pkg/tui/fields/confirm.go with Confirm struct
NewConfirm accepts title/question string
Add WithSummary(string) for additional context
View renders title and summary text
View renders Yes and No as selectable buttons
View highlights current selection
Verify: Run confirm and see question with Yes/No buttons

description: As a user, I want to toggle between Yes/No with arrow keys so that I can change my choice
steps: Handle left arrow to select No (or toggle)
Handle right arrow to select Yes (or toggle)
Visual highlight moves between Yes and No
Verify: Press left/right arrows and see highlight move

description: As a user, I want to use y/n keys as shortcuts so that I can confirm quickly
steps: Handle 'y' key to select Yes AND immediately submit
Handle 'n' key to select No AND immediately submit
Shortcuts work regardless of current highlight position
Verify: Press 'y' and confirm completes with true; press 'n' completes with false

description: As a user, I want to submit my choice with Enter so that I can proceed or cancel
steps: Handle Enter key in Update
If Yes selected: set complete=true, value=true, return nextStepMsg
If No selected: set complete=true, value=false, return cancelMsg or appropriate signal
Verify: Select Yes and Enter proceeds; select No and Enter cancels wizard

description: As a TUI developer, I want Confirm to implement Field interface so that it integrates with Wizard
steps: Implement Focus(), Blur() methods
Implement IsComplete(), GetValue() returning bool
Implement WithTheme()
Verify: Confirm satisfies Field interface (compiler check)

description: As a user, I want to see one step at a time so that I can focus on the current question
steps: Wizard.View() calls View() only on current step's Field
Previous and next steps are not rendered
Render help text showing ESC/Enter/Ctrl+C navigation
Verify: Run wizard with multiple steps, only one step visible at a time

description: As a user, I want to advance to the next step when I complete the current one so that I can progress through the wizard
steps: Wizard.Update handles nextStepMsg from fields
On nextStepMsg: call Blur() on current field
Extract value from current field and store in WorkflowState
Increment current step index
Call Focus() on new current field
Verify: Complete step 1, see step 2 appear with correct focus

description: As a user, I want to go back with ESC so that I can change a previous answer
steps: Wizard.Update handles ESC key (tea.KeyEsc)
If not on first step: decrement current index
Call Focus() on previous step's field
Previous field retains its value for editing
If on first step: ESC does nothing (or cancels)
Verify: Advance to step 2, press ESC, see step 1 with original value

description: As a user, I want to cancel entirely with Ctrl+C so that I can abort the wizard
steps: Wizard.Update handles Ctrl+C (tea.KeyCtrlC)
Set wizard cancelled flag to true
Return tea.Quit command
Wizard.IsCancelled() returns true after
Verify: Press Ctrl+C at any step, wizard exits, IsCancelled() is true

description: As a user, I want irrelevant steps to be skipped automatically so that I only answer necessary questions
steps: When advancing: check next step's Skip function if non-nil
If Skip(state) returns true, advance to next non-skipped step
When going back: skip over steps whose Skip returns true
Handle edge case where all remaining steps are skipped
Verify: Configure step with Skip returning true, confirm it's never shown

description: As a user, I want the wizard to complete when I finish all steps so that I see the result
steps: Detect when nextStepMsg received on last step
Set wizard complete flag to true
Return tea.Quit command
Wizard.IsComplete() returns true
Wizard.State() returns final WorkflowState with all values
Verify: Complete all steps, wizard exits, IsComplete() true, State() populated

description: As a user, I want the wizard to adapt to terminal resize so that layout remains usable
steps: Wizard.Update handles tea.WindowSizeMsg
Update Context.Width and Context.Height
Propagate dimensions to current field (via WithWidth/WithHeight or re-focus)
Verify: Run wizard, resize terminal, UI adapts without breaking

description: As a TUI developer, I want an Eval type so that I can manage async data fetching declaratively
steps: Create pkg/tui/eval.go with Eval[T any] generic struct
Eval holds: value T, fetch func() (T, error), loaded bool, loading bool, err error
Implement Get() that returns cached value if loaded
If not loaded and not loading, trigger async fetch
Verify: Create Eval with fetch func, call Get(), value eventually populated

description: As a user, I want to see a loading indicator while data loads so that I know something is happening
steps: Eval.IsLoading() returns true while fetch in progress
Field checks IsLoading() in View() to show spinner
Add brief delay before showing spinner (avoid flicker for fast loads)
Verify: Create slow fetch, see spinner appear, spinner disappears when loaded

description: As a user, I want loaded data to be cached so that I don't wait repeatedly
steps: After successful fetch, set loaded=true and store value
Subsequent Get() calls return cached value immediately
Fetch function not called again if already loaded
Verify: Call Get() twice, confirm fetch only runs once

description: As a TUI developer, I want to invalidate cached data so that fresh data can be fetched
steps: Implement Invalidate() method on Eval
Invalidate sets loaded=false and clears cached value
Next Get() triggers fresh fetch
Verify: Load data, invalidate, Get() again, confirm fetch runs twice

description: As a TUI developer, I want Filterable to support async options so that JIRA issues can load dynamically
steps: Add WithOptionsFunc(func() ([]Option, error)) to Filterable
Create internal Eval to manage the async options
In View(), show spinner if options loading
When loaded, display options in list
Verify: Create Filterable with async options, see spinner then options

description: As a user, I want active fields to be visually prominent so that I know where my input goes
steps: After Focus(), field uses Theme.Focused styles
Title rendered with Focused.Title style (bold/bright)
Input area rendered with Focused.Input style
Cursor is visible in focused state
Verify: Focus a field, confirm it appears bold/highlighted

description: As a user, I want inactive fields to be visually muted so that I can distinguish them
steps: After Blur(), field uses Theme.Blurred styles
Title rendered with Blurred.Title style (dimmed)
Input area rendered with Blurred.Input style
Verify: Blur a field, confirm it appears dimmed compared to focused

description: As a TUI developer, I want sensible default styles so that the TUI looks good without configuration
steps: DefaultTheme() returns complete Theme with all styles set
Focused styles use bright/bold colors
Blurred styles use muted colors
Colors should work on both light and dark terminal backgrounds
Verify: Use DefaultTheme, run wizard, UI is readable and styled

description: As a user creating a feature worktree, I want to follow the correct step sequence so that I provide all required information
steps: Create FeatureWorkflow function returning configured Wizard
Step 1: Selector with 'Feature' option (or shared workflow type step)
Step 2: Filterable for JIRA/worktree name
Step 3: TextInput for branch name
Step 4: Filterable for base branch (with Skip condition)
Step 5: Confirm
Verify: Run feature workflow, steps appear in correct order

description: As a user creating a feature worktree, I want a sensible branch name generated so that I don't have to type it
steps: After worktree name step, generate default branch name
Format: feature/{issue-key}-{slugified-summary}
Slug: lowercase, spaces to hyphens, special chars removed
Set as default value for branch name TextInput
Verify: Select JIRA issue, branch name auto-fills with feature/KEY-slug format

description: As a user creating a feature worktree, I want to skip base branch selection if the branch exists so that I save time
steps: Base branch step has Skip function checking branch existence
Skip calls GitService.BranchExists(state.BranchName)
If exists: Skip returns true, step is skipped
If not exists: Skip returns false, step is shown
Verify: Enter existing branch name, base branch step skipped; enter new name, step shown

description: As a user creating a feature worktree, I want the worktree named after my JIRA issue so that it's easy to identify
steps: When JIRA issue selected, extract issue key as worktree name
Store issue key (e.g., 'INGSVC-6468') in WorkflowState.WorktreeName
If custom text entered, use that as worktree name
Verify: Select JIRA issue, WorktreeName equals issue key

description: As a user creating a feature worktree, I want to see a summary before creation so that I can verify my choices
steps: Confirm step displays: 'Create branch {BranchName}?'
If base branch selected, show: 'From base: {BaseBranch}'
Show worktree name in summary
Verify: Reach confirm step, see branch name, base branch, worktree name

description: As a user creating a hotfix worktree, I want to follow the correct step sequence so that hotfixes are based on production
steps: Create HotfixWorkflow function returning configured Wizard
Step 1: Selector with 'Hotfix' option (or shared workflow type step)
Step 2: Filterable for JIRA/worktree name
Step 3: Filterable for base branch (NO Skip - always shown)
Step 4: TextInput for branch name
Step 5: Confirm
Verify: Run hotfix workflow, base branch step appears BEFORE branch name

description: As a user creating a hotfix worktree, I want the worktree prefixed with HOTFIX_ so that hotfixes are visually distinct
steps: After JIRA/worktree name selected, prepend 'HOTFIX_'
Example: 'INGSVC-6468' becomes 'HOTFIX_INGSVC-6468'
Apply prefix to both JIRA-selected and custom names
Verify: Select JIRA issue, WorktreeName is 'HOTFIX_' + key

description: As a user creating a hotfix worktree, I want a hotfix/ branch prefix so that branches follow naming convention
steps: Generate default branch name with hotfix/ prefix
Format: hotfix/{issue-key}-{slugified-summary}
Same slug generation logic as feature workflow
Verify: Select JIRA issue in hotfix flow, branch name starts with hotfix/

description: As a user creating a hotfix worktree, I want to always select a base branch so that I choose the correct production branch
steps: Base branch step in hotfix workflow has no Skip function (or Skip always returns false)
Prompt text suggests production/release branches
User must explicitly select a base branch
Verify: Run hotfix workflow, base branch step always appears

description: As a user creating a hotfix worktree, I want to see a summary before creation so that I can verify my choices
steps: Confirm step displays: 'Create branch {BranchName}?'
Show: 'From base: {BaseBranch}'
Show worktree name (with HOTFIX_ prefix)
Verify: Reach confirm step, see hotfix branch name, base branch, prefixed worktree name

description: As a developer, I want a test command to launch the wizard so that I can try it without affecting real repos
steps: Add 'testadd' subcommand to 'gbm wt' command group
Command creates Context with mock Git and JIRA services
Command creates Wizard with workflow steps
Run wizard with tea.NewProgram using WithAltScreen
Use /dev/tty for input to support piped scenarios
Verify: Run 'gbm wt testadd', wizard UI appears

description: As a developer, I want mock JIRA data so that I can test the JIRA selector without real API calls
steps: Create MockJiraService implementing JiraService interface
FetchIssues returns static slice of 5-10 fake issues
Add configurable delay to simulate network latency
Issues have realistic keys and summaries
Verify: Wizard shows fake JIRA issues in filterable list

description: As a developer, I want mock Git data so that I can test branch selection without real repos
steps: Create MockGitService implementing GitService interface
ListBranches returns static slice of fake branches
Include mix: main, master, feature/*, production-*
BranchExists returns configurable result (default false)
Verify: Wizard shows fake branches in base branch selector

description: As a user, I want to see what would be created when the wizard completes so that I can verify without side effects
steps: After wizard.IsComplete(), print summary to stderr
Show: 'Would create worktree: {WorktreeName}'
Show: 'Would create branch: {BranchName}'
Show: 'Based on: {BaseBranch}' (if applicable)
Do NOT actually create worktree or branch
Verify: Complete wizard, see dry-run summary, no files created

description: As a user, I want cancellation to exit cleanly so that no mess is left behind
steps: If wizard.IsCancelled(), print 'Cancelled' to stderr
Exit with code 1 (or appropriate non-zero)
No partial WorkflowState printed
No files or state created
Verify: Press Ctrl+C, see 'Cancelled' message, exit code non-zero
